text:
HIGH — Still Exploitable or Data-Losing
1. SoftDeletes missing on 5 models
These models can be permanently deleted with no recovery path and no trash audit:

Model	Status
SafetyIncident	❌ No SoftDeletes
CrewAttendance	❌ No SoftDeletes
DailySiteDiary	❌ No SoftDeletes
EquipmentAllocation	❌ No SoftDeletes
EquipmentFuelLog	❌ No SoftDeletes
Fix: One migration + add use SoftDeletes to each model. Also register them in CompanyTrash so admins can restore from the Trash page.

2. Performance indexes migration still Pending
2026_03_29_002650_add_performance_indexes_to_core_tables  → Pending
This migration exists but hasn't been run. On a production database with thousands of rows, queries on tasks, invoices, expenses without indexes on company_id + cde_project_id will be painfully slow. Run php artisan migrate on the production server.

3. Client portal login has no rate limiting
The Filament client panel login (/client/login) goes through Filament's built-in auth — but there's no throttle applied to it. The main API login has throttle:5,1 but the client Filament panel does not. A brute-force attack on client passwords is possible.

Fix: In ClientPanelProvider, add a login rate limiter.

🟠 MEDIUM — Operational Risk
4. ChangeOrder model has no SoftDeletes
The migration add_soft_deletes_to_change_orders_table exists (from Feb) but let's verify ChangeOrder model actually uses the trait:

php
// ChangeOrder.php — check if `use SoftDeletes` is inside the class
Your open file suggests it might not have it. Worth verifying.

5. Excel Schedule Import — no start date calculation from predecessors
The importer I built today assigns sequential dates (task N starts after task N-1 ends). In reality, the schedule has dependency chains that mean many tasks can run in parallel. For proper Gantt display, a CPM (Critical Path Method) forward-pass is needed after import.

Fix: After inserting all tasks, run a CPM forward-pass using the TaskDependency records to compute actual start_date/due_date for each task.

6. ExportsTableCsv trait — no company scope enforcement
Referenced in your round 3 audit — the trait used for CSV exports on resource tables doesn't verify that the data being exported belongs to the current company. A malicious user could potentially call the export endpoint with a manipulated tableFilters payload.

7. Mobile layout (/mobile/layout.blade.php) — your current open file
If there's a mobile API / PWA layer that does its own data fetching, ensure those routes go through the same tenant middleware as the main app.

🟡 LOW — Polish & Future-Proofing
8. ScheduleExcelImportService — import dates should respect project's actual start
Currently defaults to today. The schedule file itself has no absolute dates (only durations + predecessors), so the start date picker in the modal is the right UX — but consider defaulting it to the project's own start_date field if it's set.

9. Notification cleanup — notifications table grows unbounded
The notifications table accumulates read notifications forever. Add a scheduled cleanup:

php
// Monthly: delete read notifications older than 90 days
DB::table('notifications')->whereNotNull('read_at')->where('read_at', '<', now()->subDays(90))->delete();
10. Gantt refresh after Excel import — may need a page reload
The $this->dispatch('gantt-refresh') event works if the Gantt JS listens for Livewire events. If dates shift significantly after a CPM recalculation, a hard page reload ($this->redirect(request()->url())) may be more reliable.

✅ What's Already Well-Covered
16 Policies + EnforcesCompanyOwnership trait ✅
ReportingPage double-scoped queries ✅
canCreate() subscription guards ✅
persistFiltersInSession on all 7 resources ✅
Activity log pruning scheduled ✅
Billing idempotency via updateOrCreate ✅
API auth throttling ✅
withoutGlobalScopes on console commands ✅
Recommended Order of Work
1. php artisan migrate              ← run pending indexes NOW
2. SoftDeletes on 5 models          ← 1 migration, 5 model edits
3. Client portal login rate limit   ← 5-minute fix in ClientPanelProvider
4. CPM forward-pass on Excel import ← improves Gantt accuracy
5. Notifications table cleanup      ← add to scheduler
6. ExportsTableCsv company guard    ← close last data-leakage vector
